externalControllers: reuse existing core persistFn wiring rather than duplicating it
memoryController: persist AgentMemorySnapshot resources returned by dispatch or snapshot creation
persist AgentRunMemoryImport resources returned by createMemoryImport
persist AgentMemoryUpdate resources returned by createMemoryUpdate
report apply failures instead of returning apparent success
modelRoutes: apply generated AIGatewayRoute manifests for persisted KradleModelRoute resources
prefer an existing resource gateway/controller apply boundary over route-side kubectl shelling
surface manifest apply failures to the caller or observable status

knownAlreadyHandled: createKradleApiController already constructs external sync/write/conflict controllers with persistFn callbacks.
packages/kradle/core/src/http-server.js already forwards full external sync options and object-shaped write-intent approve arguments.
model-route-controller already validates KradleModelRoute resources and generates valid AIGatewayRoute manifests; the missing piece is applying them.
Issue comments mention a prior planning/implementation PR; rerun the runtime trace on the current base branch before changing code so already-applied fixes are not duplicated.
acceptanceCriteria: External sync POST through the live web route forwards bindingName, kind, localName, spec, externalEnvelope, and watermark to syncExternalBinding.
External write-intent approve through the live web route calls approveExternalWriteIntent with { intentName, approvedBy, resources }.
External conflict resolve remains covered so resolved conflicts are persisted through existing core persistFn wiring.
AgentMemorySnapshot, AgentRunMemoryImport, and AgentMemoryUpdate resources created by API/dispatch flows are applied durably in the org namespace.
Memory persistence apply failures are returned as explicit API/controller failures rather than silent success.
Creating or reconciling a KradleModelRoute generates and applies the matching AIGatewayRoute manifest through existing Kubernetes resource application infrastructure.
Model-route manifest apply failures are observable and covered by tests.
No route-side direct kubectl shell invocation is introduced.
Existing org-scoping, cache invalidation, audit/event emission, and resource validation behavior remain intact.
qualityGates: Reuse audit completed before new persistence abstractions are introduced.
Runtime call paths from web/API entry points to applyResource or resourceGateway.apply are recorded.
Failing tests are authored before production code changes.
Targeted tests and the relevant SDK/web contract tests pass after implementation.
Code review approves persistence semantics, error propagation, idempotency, org scoping, and Kubernetes safety.
Final acceptance matrix maps each #610 requirement to changed files and fresh verification evidence.
processLibraryReferences: active babysitter capability guidance for branch policy, PR lifecycle, and issue linking

delivery.prTitle: Fix controller persistence wiring for external sync, memory, and model routes
